evals.name: reflection-default-monolith
evals.prompt: I've been telling my team we should kill our microservices and collapse back to a monolith. I'm fairly convinced I'm right, but I want it pressure-tested before the architecture review. Spin up two agents — one defending the idea, one challenging it with fresh eyes — run it about 6 turns, and show me the transcript plus the session ids so I can dig in later.
evals.expected_output: Uses codex-duo in default reflection mode: A=proponent articulates+defends the monolith idea and honestly concedes/refines under pressure; B=socratic challenges cold with no priors. ~6 alternating turns. Returns transcript + per-agent thread_ids.

evals.name: custom-debate-rlhf
evals.prompt: Set up a debate between two codex agents on whether RLHF is sufficient for AI alignment — one arguing strongly yes, one strongly no. Go about 6 exchanges and give me the whole back-and-forth.
evals.expected_output: Uses codex-duo with custom --system-a/--system-b personas (pro vs con). ~6 alternating turns of substantive, position-held debate. Returns full transcript + metadata.

evals.name: minimal-challenge-db-per-service
evals.prompt: challenge this belief for me with codex-duo: 'every microservice should own its own database.' just a few turns, read-only
evals.expected_output: Uses codex-duo default reflection mode, short turn count, --sandbox read-only. A defends the claim, B challenges Socratically. Returns transcript + metadata.
